repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Hello, this is my first contribution, and I pulled this issue as it seemed relatively easy and scoped to Lua.

## Problem

`get_node_text()` returned inconsistent results between buffer and string sources when a node's range ends at `end_col == 0` (i.e. the node ends with a newline). The buffer path dropped the trailing newline; the string path included it correctly.

## Solution

Append `'\n'` in `buf_range_get_text()` when `end_col == 0` and `start_row ~= end_row`. The `start_row ~= end_row` guard excludes zero-width nodes at column 0, which should return `""`.

Remove the workaround in the `#trim!` directive that manually compensated for the missing newline.

Strip whitespace in `resolve_lang()` so injection language nodes ending at `end_col == 0` (e.g. `">lua\n"`) still resolve correctly.

Fixes #28677

comment_data.body: We now have to strip whitespace here, since language names can come back as like `"lua\n"` here since `get_text_node()` now includes it.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1023,7 +1023,7 @@ end)
 ---@return string? # resolved parser name
 local function resolve_lang(alias)
   -- normalize: treesitter language names are always lower case and use underscores
-  alias = alias and alias:lower():gsub('%-', '_')
+  alias = alias and alias:gsub('%s+', ''):lower():gsub('%-', '_')

comment_data.body: Had to update this assertion to match the new correct behavior.
This test passed when I added it, so the string source codepath was already doing the right thing here. Having a test here explicitly seemed good.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -227,6 +227,32 @@ describe('treesitter node API', function()
     eq({ 0, 0, 2, 3 }, lua_eval('range'))
   end)
 
+  it(
+    'get_node_text() includes trailing newline for buffer source when node end_col == 0',
+    function()
+      insert('local x = 1')
+
+      exec_lua(function()
+        _G.node = vim.treesitter.get_parser(0, 'lua'):parse()[1]:root()
+      end)
+
+      eq('local x = 1\n', lua_eval('vim.treesitter.get_node_text(_G.node, 0)'))
+    end
+  )
+
+  it(
+    'get_node_text() includes trailing newline for string source when node end_col == 0',
+    function()
+      exec_lua(function()
+        local source = 'local x = 1\n'
+        _G.source = source
+        _G.node = vim.treesitter.get_string_parser(source, 'lua'):parse()[1]:root()
+      end)
+
+      eq('local x = 1\n', lua_eval('vim.treesitter.get_node_text(_G.node, _G.source)'))
+    end
+  )

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
  -- Language name (from `get_text_node`) may end with "\n".
  alias = alias and alias:gsub('%s+', ''):lower():gsub('%-', '_')
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1023,7 +1023,7 @@ end)
 ---@return string? # resolved parser name
 local function resolve_lang(alias)
   -- normalize: treesitter language names are always lower case and use underscores
-  alias = alias and alias:lower():gsub('%-', '_')
+  alias = alias and alias:gsub('%s+', ''):lower():gsub('%-', '_')

comment_data.body:
nit: this might save some string work:


```suggestion
  if append_newline then
    table.insert(lines, '')
  end
  local text = table.concat(lines, '\n')
  return text
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -210,7 +211,11 @@ local function buf_range_get_text(buf, range)
     end_row = end_row - 1
   end
   local lines = api.nvim_buf_get_text(buf, start_row, start_col, end_row, end_col, {})
-  return table.concat(lines, '\n')
+  local text = table.concat(lines, '\n')
+  if append_newline then
+    text = text .. '\n'
+  end
+  return text

@@ -209,7 +211,13 @@ local function buf_range_get_text(buf, range)
     end_col = -1
     end_row = end_row - 1
   end
+
   local lines = api.nvim_buf_get_text(buf, start_row, start_col, end_row, end_col, {})
+

comment_regular_data.body: this looks great, thanks! why still in Draft?

hey @justinmk! it was still in draft because GitHub Actions got stuck queuing for runners for a while and now CI has failed on a few jobs. I wanted to get CI passing before I bugged you for a review, but thanks for reviewing despite that! I'll address comments and CI next.
@wookayin done! `with-external-deps` seems to be failing, but it also is failing on `master`...
LMK if you want to fix the `master` build first, and I can rebase on it here. Or if this is a known CI issue, maybe you can merge anyways -- up to you!
Looks like a network flake; we'll just retry it later.
Yeah, hopefully it clears up. All these builds seem affected too: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/commits/master/

Yeah, this is an upstream issue (https://ppa.launchpadcontent.net/neovim-ppa/stable/ubuntu/ is not accessible). It doesn't look like we actually need the PPA currently; let's try https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39557.
Rebased on the `neovim/master` fix, if CI passes should be good.
Hm, I think CI has flaky tests.
If the test flakes are tracked somewhere I could take a look?

> If the test flakes are tracked somewhere I could take a look?

That would be very appreciated! Tracked here: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20state%3Aopen%20in%3Atitle%20unreliable

title: fix(treesitter): get_node_text() should include trailing newline for buffer source
